description: Staff Management Backend - .NET 10 Web API for managing staff members with CRUD operations, role assignment, weekly schedule storage as JSON, and explicit deactivation/reactivation following ADR-009 timestamps pattern

userStories.title: StaffMember entity, EF Core configuration, and migration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a StaffMember entity with EF Core mapping and a database migration so that staff data can be persisted.
acceptanceCriteria: src/backend/Chairly.Domain/Enums/StaffRole.cs created with: public enum StaffRole { Manager, StaffMember } — note: classification enum, not affected by ADR-009

src/backend/Chairly.Domain/Entities/StaffMember.cs created with properties: Id (Guid), TenantId (Guid), FirstName (string), LastName (string), Role (StaffRole), Color (string, hex color), PhotoUrl (string?), ScheduleJson (string, stores JSON of weekly schedule, default '{}'), DeactivatedAtUtc (DateTimeOffset?, null = active per ADR-009), DeactivatedBy (Guid?, null = active), CreatedAtUtc (DateTimeOffset), CreatedBy (Guid), UpdatedAtUtc (DateTimeOffset?), UpdatedBy (Guid?)
.gitkeep removed from Chairly.Domain/Enums/ and Chairly.Domain/Entities/

src/backend/Chairly.Infrastructure/Persistence/Configurations/StaffMemberConfiguration.cs created implementing IEntityTypeConfiguration<StaffMember> with: HasKey on Id, TenantId required, FirstName required MaxLength(100), LastName required MaxLength(100), Color required MaxLength(20), PhotoUrl optional MaxLength(500), ScheduleJson required HasColumnType('text'), CreatedBy required, DeactivatedBy optional, UpdatedBy optional, index on (FirstName, LastName, TenantId) not unique
ChairlyDbContext.cs updated: add 'public DbSet<StaffMember> StaffMembers => Set<StaffMember>();'

EF Core migration created named 'AddStaffMember' by running: dotnet ef migrations add AddStaffMember -p src/backend/Chairly.Infrastructure/Chairly.Infrastructure.csproj -s src/backend/Chairly.Api/Chairly.Api.csproj (run from repo root)
dotnet build src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet test src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet format src/backend/Chairly.slnx --verify-no-changes passes
userStories.notes: Follow the Service entity pattern in Chairly.Domain/Entities/Service.cs and ServiceConfiguration in Infrastructure. StaffRole is a classification enum (see ADR-009: 'Classification enums are not affected by this ADR — they represent type, not lifecycle'). IsActive is NOT a stored field — it is derived from DeactivatedAtUtc (null = active). ScheduleJson stores the weekly schedule as a plain JSON string (not a JSON column type). If the dotnet-ef tool is not installed, run: dotnet tool install --global dotnet-ef first. The connection string for design-time migration generation may come from appsettings.json or environment. If migration creation fails due to missing connection string, add a design-time factory: create src/backend/Chairly.Infrastructure/ChairlyDbContextFactory.cs implementing IDesignTimeDbContextFactory<ChairlyDbContext> that creates a context with UseNpgsql('Host=localhost;Database=chairly;Username=chairly;Password=chairly').

userStories.title: StaffMemberResponse DTO and GetStaffList endpoint
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I need a GET /api/staff endpoint that returns all staff members so the frontend can display the staff list.
acceptanceCriteria: src/backend/Chairly.Api/Features/Staff/ShiftBlockResponse.cs created: internal sealed record ShiftBlockResponse(string StartTime, string EndTime)

src/backend/Chairly.Api/Features/Staff/StaffMemberResponse.cs created: internal sealed record StaffMemberResponse(Guid Id, string FirstName, string LastName, string Role, string Color, string? PhotoUrl, bool IsActive, Dictionary<string, ShiftBlockResponse[]> Schedule, DateTimeOffset CreatedAtUtc, DateTimeOffset? UpdatedAtUtc)
src/backend/Chairly.Api/Features/Staff/GetStaffList/GetStaffListQuery.cs created: internal sealed record GetStaffListQuery() : IRequest<IEnumerable<StaffMemberResponse>>

src/backend/Chairly.Api/Features/Staff/GetStaffList/GetStaffListHandler.cs created: queries db.StaffMembers where TenantId == TenantConstants.DefaultTenantId, orders by FirstName then LastName, maps to StaffMemberResponse. Role is mapped by converting the enum value: StaffRole.Manager -> 'manager', StaffRole.StaffMember -> 'staff_member'. IsActive = (entity.DeactivatedAtUtc == null). Schedule = deserialize ScheduleJson using System.Text.Json.JsonSerializer.Deserialize<Dictionary<string, ShiftBlockResponse[]>>(entity.ScheduleJson) ?? new Dictionary<string, ShiftBlockResponse[]>()
src/backend/Chairly.Api/Features/Staff/GetStaffList/GetStaffListEndpoint.cs created: MapGet on '/' returning Results.Ok(result)

src/backend/Chairly.Api/Features/Staff/StaffEndpoints.cs created: internal static class StaffEndpoints with MapStaffEndpoints extension method that creates group on '/api/staff' and calls MapGetStaffList()
Program.cs updated: add 'app.MapStaffEndpoints();' after app.MapServiceEndpoints()

src/backend/Chairly.Tests/Features/Staff/StaffHandlerTests.cs created with test: GetStaffListHandler_HappyPath_ReturnsListOrderedByFirstNameLastName — seeds two StaffMembers and asserts count and ordering
dotnet build src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet test src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet format src/backend/Chairly.slnx --verify-no-changes passes
userStories.notes: Follow the GetServicesListHandler pattern in Chairly.Api/Features/Services/GetServicesList/. Use TenantConstants.DefaultTenantId (from Chairly.Api.Shared.Tenancy) for tenant filtering. Role enum-to-string mapping: use a switch expression — StaffRole.Manager => 'manager', StaffRole.StaffMember => 'staff_member'. For ScheduleJson deserialization: the JSON is stored as a plain text string. Use JsonSerializer.Deserialize with default options (camelCase from ASP.NET Core is for HTTP responses, not for stored JSON — use default options for round-trip). The ScheduleJson stores keys as day names ('monday', 'tuesday', etc.) and values as arrays of {startTime, endTime} objects stored with camelCase keys. Use JsonSerializerOptions with PropertyNameCaseInsensitive = true to handle this. The .gitkeep in Features/Staff/ should be deleted when real files are added.

userStories.title: CreateStaffMember endpoint
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I need a POST /api/staff endpoint to create a new staff member.
acceptanceCriteria: src/backend/Chairly.Api/Features/Staff/CreateStaffMember/ShiftBlockCommand.cs created: internal sealed record ShiftBlockCommand(string StartTime, string EndTime)

src/backend/Chairly.Api/Features/Staff/CreateStaffMember/CreateStaffMemberCommand.cs created implementing IRequest<StaffMemberResponse> with properties: FirstName (string, [Required][MaxLength(100)]), LastName (string, [Required][MaxLength(100)]), Role (string, [Required][AllowedValues('manager','staff_member')]), Color (string, [Required][MaxLength(20)]), PhotoUrl (string?, [MaxLength(500)]), Schedule (Dictionary<string, ShiftBlockCommand[]>?, nullable — empty schedule if not provided)
#pragma warning disable CA1812 on command class (ASP.NET Core model binding)

src/backend/Chairly.Api/Features/Staff/CreateStaffMember/CreateStaffMemberHandler.cs created: creates StaffMember entity with TenantId=TenantConstants.DefaultTenantId, Id=Guid.NewGuid(), parses Role string to StaffRole enum, serializes Schedule dict to ScheduleJson using System.Text.Json.JsonSerializer.Serialize, sets CreatedAtUtc=DateTimeOffset.UtcNow, CreatedBy=Guid.Empty (with MA0026 pragma), IsActive derived from null DeactivatedAtUtc. Returns StaffMemberResponse.
src/backend/Chairly.Api/Features/Staff/CreateStaffMember/CreateStaffMemberEndpoint.cs created: MapPost on '/' returning Results.Created('/api/staff/{id}', result)
StaffEndpoints.cs updated: add group.MapCreateStaffMember()

src/backend/Chairly.Tests/Features/Staff/StaffHandlerTests.cs updated with tests: CreateStaffMemberHandler_HappyPath_CreatesWithCorrectRole — creates a member with role 'manager' and asserts Role==StaffRole.Manager in DB. CreateStaffMemberHandler_HappyPath_StoresScheduleJson — creates with a schedule and asserts ScheduleJson is non-empty. CreateStaffMemberCommand_EmptyFirstName_FailsValidation. CreateStaffMemberCommand_InvalidRole_FailsValidation.
dotnet build src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet test src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet format src/backend/Chairly.slnx --verify-no-changes passes
userStories.notes: Follow CreateServiceHandler pattern. Role parsing: 'manager' => StaffRole.Manager, 'staff_member' => StaffRole.StaffMember — use a switch expression. [AllowedValues] is available in .NET 8+ via System.ComponentModel.DataAnnotations. For schedule serialization, use JsonSerializer.Serialize(command.Schedule ?? new Dictionary<string, ShiftBlockCommand[]>()). The ScheduleJson round-trip: store with camelCase keys (the default System.Text.Json behavior for C# record properties). When deserializing in the handler/read side, use PropertyNameCaseInsensitive=true.

userStories.title: UpdateStaffMember endpoint
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I need a PUT /api/staff/{id} endpoint to update an existing staff member's details.
acceptanceCriteria: src/backend/Chairly.Api/Features/Staff/UpdateStaffMember/UpdateStaffMemberCommand.cs created implementing IRequest<OneOf<StaffMemberResponse, NotFound>> with Id (Guid), FirstName ([Required][MaxLength(100)]), LastName ([Required][MaxLength(100)]), Role ([Required][AllowedValues('manager','staff_member')]), Color ([Required][MaxLength(20)]), PhotoUrl (string?, [MaxLength(500)]), Schedule (Dictionary<string, ShiftBlockCommand[]>?)

src/backend/Chairly.Api/Features/Staff/UpdateStaffMember/UpdateStaffMemberHandler.cs created: finds by Id and TenantId, returns NotFound if missing, updates all fields (FirstName, LastName, Role, Color, PhotoUrl, ScheduleJson), sets UpdatedAtUtc=DateTimeOffset.UtcNow, UpdatedBy=Guid.Empty (MA0026 pragma), saves, returns StaffMemberResponse

src/backend/Chairly.Api/Features/Staff/UpdateStaffMember/UpdateStaffMemberEndpoint.cs created: MapPut on '/{id:guid}', binds Guid id from route and UpdateStaffMemberCommand from body, sets command.Id = id before sending, returns Results.Ok on success, Results.NotFound on NotFound
StaffEndpoints.cs updated: add group.MapUpdateStaffMember()

src/backend/Chairly.Tests/Features/Staff/StaffHandlerTests.cs updated with tests: UpdateStaffMemberHandler_HappyPath_UpdatesFieldsAndSetsUpdatedAtUtc. UpdateStaffMemberHandler_NotFound_ReturnsNotFound.
dotnet build src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet test src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet format src/backend/Chairly.slnx --verify-no-changes passes
userStories.notes: Follow UpdateServiceHandler/UpdateServiceCommand pattern. The endpoint receives both a route {id} and a request body — set command.Id = id in the endpoint lambda before calling mediator.Send(command). Reuse ShiftBlockCommand from the CreateStaffMember slice (import it). UpdatedAtUtc is always set on update. IsActive state (DeactivatedAtUtc) is NOT changed by this endpoint — only deactivate/reactivate endpoints touch it.

userStories.title: DeactivateStaffMember and ReactivateStaffMember endpoints
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I need PATCH /api/staff/{id}/deactivate and PATCH /api/staff/{id}/reactivate endpoints to manage staff member active status.
acceptanceCriteria: src/backend/Chairly.Api/Features/Staff/DeactivateStaffMember/DeactivateStaffMemberCommand.cs created: internal sealed record DeactivateStaffMemberCommand(Guid Id) : IRequest<OneOf<StaffMemberResponse, NotFound>>

src/backend/Chairly.Api/Features/Staff/DeactivateStaffMember/DeactivateStaffMemberHandler.cs created: finds by Id+TenantId, returns NotFound if missing, sets DeactivatedAtUtc=DateTimeOffset.UtcNow and DeactivatedBy=Guid.Empty (MA0026 pragma), sets UpdatedAtUtc=DateTimeOffset.UtcNow and UpdatedBy=Guid.Empty, saves, returns StaffMemberResponse with IsActive=false
src/backend/Chairly.Api/Features/Staff/DeactivateStaffMember/DeactivateStaffMemberEndpoint.cs created: MapPatch on '/{id:guid}/deactivate', returns Results.Ok on success, Results.NotFound on NotFound

src/backend/Chairly.Api/Features/Staff/ReactivateStaffMember/ReactivateStaffMemberCommand.cs created: internal sealed record ReactivateStaffMemberCommand(Guid Id) : IRequest<OneOf<StaffMemberResponse, NotFound>>

src/backend/Chairly.Api/Features/Staff/ReactivateStaffMember/ReactivateStaffMemberHandler.cs created: finds by Id+TenantId, returns NotFound if missing, sets DeactivatedAtUtc=null and DeactivatedBy=null, sets UpdatedAtUtc=DateTimeOffset.UtcNow and UpdatedBy=Guid.Empty (MA0026 pragma), saves, returns StaffMemberResponse with IsActive=true
src/backend/Chairly.Api/Features/Staff/ReactivateStaffMember/ReactivateStaffMemberEndpoint.cs created: MapPatch on '/{id:guid}/reactivate', returns Results.Ok on success, Results.NotFound on NotFound
StaffEndpoints.cs updated: add group.MapDeactivateStaffMember() and group.MapReactivateStaffMember()

src/backend/Chairly.Tests/Features/Staff/StaffHandlerTests.cs updated with tests: DeactivateStaffMemberHandler_HappyPath_SetsDeactivatedAtUtc (asserts IsActive=false and DeactivatedAtUtc non-null). DeactivateStaffMemberHandler_NotFound_ReturnsNotFound. ReactivateStaffMemberHandler_HappyPath_ClearsDeactivatedAtUtc (asserts IsActive=true and DeactivatedAtUtc null). ReactivateStaffMemberHandler_NotFound_ReturnsNotFound.
dotnet build src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet test src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet format src/backend/Chairly.slnx --verify-no-changes passes
userStories.notes: Follow the ToggleServiceActive pattern but as two separate endpoints (not toggle). Deactivate sets DeactivatedAtUtc/DeactivatedBy to values; reactivate sets them to null. Both also update UpdatedAtUtc/UpdatedBy. The response IsActive is computed: entity.DeactivatedAtUtc == null. The frontend calls PATCH /api/staff/{id}/deactivate and PATCH /api/staff/{id}/reactivate (matching the StaffApiService in libs/chairly/src/lib/staff/data-access/staff-api.service.ts). After completing this story, ALL stories are complete — follow the COMPLETE protocol in CLAUDE.md: push, create PR, wait for CI.
